title: Help understanding poor multithreaded scaling
body:
Hi,

we're using Verilator on a fairly small MCU design and I've been looking, or rather trying to look, at how to optimise our run time.  So far we're not seeing any real benefit from using multithreading and I'm trying to work out why.

The data below was compiled by running a decent sized C test on our MCU and times are in seconds.

| Threads | Execution Time |
| :--- | :---|
| 1 | 165 |
| 4 | 137 |

I've run the 4 thread case with profiling enabled and I get this output from gprof:

```
Flat profile:

Each sample counts as 0.01 seconds.
  %   cumulative   self              self     total
 time   seconds   seconds    calls   s/call   s/call  name
 67.47    198.62   198.62 16368132     0.00     0.00  VlMTaskVertex::waitUntilUpstreamDone(bool) const
  9.94    227.87    29.25  2007781     0.00     0.00  VlWorkerThread::dequeWork(VlWorkerThread::ExecRec*)
  2.58    235.46     7.59  5411940     0.00     0.00  Vchip_sim_tb___024root____Vthread__eval__1(void*, bool)
  1.65    240.32     4.87  9396749     0.00     0.00  Vchip_sim_tb___024root____Vthread__eval__2(void*, bool)
  1.59    245.00     4.68   503926     0.00     0.00  Vchip_sim_tb___024root____Vthread__eval__0(void*, bool)
  1.40    249.12     4.12   233180     0.00     0.00  Vchip_sim_tb___024root____Vthread__eval__3(void*, bool)
  0.43    250.38     1.25 15260626     0.00     0.00  VlWorkerThread::addTask(void (*)(void*, bool), void*, bool)
  0.30    251.26     0.88 14798051     0.00     0.00  VerilatedScope::exportFind(VerilatedScope const*, int)
<snip>
```

And here's the gantt chart of the threads.

![gantt](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/99925711/223432465-cc798f20-7199-4e89-b64e-080263adb163.jpg)

So where do I go from here?
